risks: No NSG resources defined for either VNet — workload subnets lack network-level segmentation, leaving VMs exposed to lateral traffic within each VNet.
No monitoring or alerting resources (Log Analytics workspace, Azure Monitor alerts) for ASR replication health — silent replication failures could go undetected until an actual failover is needed.
No ASR Recovery Plan resource (Microsoft.RecoveryServices/vaults/replicationRecoveryPlans) to orchestrate multi-VM failover and failback with sequenced steps and automation runbook hooks.

Managed identity (id-aa-dr-prod-westus-001) is not connected to the Automation Account (aa-dr-prod-westus-001) in the dependency graph or properties — the runbook will not be able to authenticate to update Traffic Manager endpoints.

No RBAC role assignment resource granting the managed identity Contributor (or Traffic Manager Contributor) on the Traffic Manager profile — the DNS failback runbook will fail at runtime without this permission.
No reverse network mapping (West US → East US) defined for failback — VMs failing back to East US may not be placed in the correct source VNet automatically.
No diagnostic settings on the Recovery Services Vault — audit and replication event logs will not be captured for operational troubleshooting.
Traffic Manager DNS failover latency (~60s with 30s TTL + 10s probe interval) may exceed RTO expectations for latency-sensitive workloads — documented as a tradeoff but worth flagging.
correctionsRecommended: Add NSG resources for snet-workload-prod-eastus-001 and snet-workload-prod-westus-001 with rules to restrict inbound/outbound traffic to required ports only.
Add a Log Analytics workspace and configure diagnostic settings on the Recovery Services Vault to capture ASR replication health events and Site Recovery alerts.
Add an ASR Recovery Plan resource (Microsoft.RecoveryServices/vaults/replicationRecoveryPlans) that groups both VMs and attaches the Automation runbook as a post-failover/post-failback action.
Add a dependency from aa-dr-prod-westus-001 to id-aa-dr-prod-westus-001 and configure the identity property on the Automation Account to reference the user-assigned managed identity.

Add a Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments resource granting the managed identity (id-aa-dr-prod-westus-001) the Traffic Manager Contributor role scoped to the Traffic Manager profile (traf-dr-prod-001).
Add a reverse network mapping resource (West US VNet → East US VNet) using asr-fabric-westus as the source fabric to support automated failback network placement.
Consider adding Azure Monitor alert rules for ASR replication RPO breaches and replication health degradation.
